edges.quote: otherwise the Subpostmaster would have to make good the loss

edges.rationale: Indicates a real loss existed at a claimant's branch which he had to address, undercutting the denial that loss was suffered.
edges.quote: alleging that substantially all loss was created by Horizon

edges.rationale: Describes altering cash declarations to keep operating, ambiguous as to actual loss suffered by claimant.
edges.quote: She denied stealing any money but explained that she had altered the cash declarations in order to continue to operate the branch

edges.rationale: Suggests an alleged shortfall may have been caused by a system error, supporting that the claimant bore an unwarranted loss.
edges.quote: whether a system error was causing the alleged shortfall

edges.rationale: Acknowledges losses commonly arose at branches due to error, supporting that losses occurred.
edges.quote: it was common for branches to have a loss of this amount due to an error

edges.rationale: Confirms a real loss appeared in cash declarations and at audit at a claimant's branch.
edges.quote: previous cash declarations had shown an initial loss which was similar to the final loss at audit

edges.rationale: Confirms an unusually high level of loss at a claimant's branch.
edges.quote: there were no other offices that I dealt with which were experiencing this level of loss.

edges.rationale: Frames losses as concealed by subpostmasters' false accounting, ambiguous on whether claimants suffered genuine loss.
edges.quote: had inflated their cash on hand figures to cover up a loss

edges.rationale: Concerns a role limited to sales and unrelated to contractual loss allocation.
edges.quote: In March 2005 my area was extended again but limited to the responsibility for sales and no contractual issues.

edges.rationale: Concerns extending the Fujitsu contract, unrelated to the subpostmaster loss allocation.
edges.quote: The business case was to extend the c ntract with Fuj,tsL on r ew terms until i° arci, 2015 and to replace the existing system

edges.rationale: Concerns a disclosure decision causing loss of opportunity, irrelevant to contractual loss allocation.
edges.quote: our decision concerning family review - for which I take full responsibility -- caused the loss of an opportunity to identify Appendix 6

edges.rationale: Concerns chasing action points, unrelated to contractual loss allocation.
edges.quote: I took responsibility for trying to resolve some action points that were raised

edges.rationale: Concerns when a witness became aware of Horizon, irrelevant to loss allocation.
edges.quote: During 2009, I became aware of the Horizon IT system by virtue of my involvement in relevant contractual work

edges.rationale: Concerns the scope of a witness's work, not contractual loss allocation.
edges.quote: My work did not extend to the original or older Horizon system (pre-2010), also referred to as 'Legacy Horizon'

edges.rationale: Limits contractual responsibility to losses caused by the subpostmaster's own negligence, carelessness or error, supporting that it did not extend to system-caused losses.
edges.quote: From a purely contractual perspective a sub postmaster or other agent is responsible for all losses caused through his own negligence, carelessness or error.

edges.rationale: Frames the contractual obligation as confined to negligence/error and assistants' losses, not losses from the Defendant's system.
edges.quote: The Contract provided that he was obliged to make good all losses caused through his own negligence, carelessness or error and losses of any kind caused by his assistants.

edges.rationale: Restricts responsibility to shortfalls caused by negligence/carelessness/error, supporting that system-caused losses fall outside the contract.
edges.quote: the subpostmaster was responsible for shortfalls or losses incurred at the branch caused through his/her negligence, carelessness or error

edges.rationale: Confines liability to the subpostmaster's own carelessness, negligence or error, consistent with the denial.
edges.quote: Sub-postmasters were liable for losses arising from their own carelessness, negligence or error, or for the same by their assistants, as set out in the relevant contracts.

edges.rationale: Defines responsibility by reference to assistants' conduct, consistent with the contract not covering system-caused losses.
edges.quote: The subpostmaster was also responsible for losses of all kinds caused by their assistants working in the branch

edges.rationale: Addresses disclosure of defect documents, not the contractual allocation of system-caused losses.
edges.quote:
Had we ever received documents that showed that defects in the Horizon IT system at the Marine Drive branch caused illusory losses during the period in
question, we would have disclosed them.

edges.rationale: Concerns belief in Horizon's robustness, not the contractual scope of loss responsibility.
edges.quote: At no time did I ever get the impression that for one minute did Ms Talbot believe that the Horizon IT system caused losses at the Marine Drive branch.

edges.rationale: References general complaints about the contractual recovery position but provides no detail on system-caused losses allocation.
edges.quote: there was a regular background level of general complaint from the NFSP about crime, losses and the POL contractual position on recoveries

edges.rationale: States POL bore responsibility for its own system, supporting that system-related responsibility did not rest with the subpostmaster.
edges.quote: The responsibility for monitoring the Horizon IT System ("Horizon") lay with POL

edges.rationale: States responsibility extended to making good any losses incurred, contradicting the claim that system-caused losses were excluded.
edges.quote: they were responsible for making good any losses incurred.

edges.rationale: Concerns which team would investigate Horizon issues, not contractual loss allocation.
edges.quote: NBSC would not have been able to identify if there were any issues caused by the Horizon system

edges.rationale: Concerns impressions about Horizon's operation, not the contractual scope of responsibility.
edges.quote: At no stage did I ever get any impression from POL or Fujitsu that caused me to believe that they thought there was anything wrong with the operation of the Horizon system

edges.rationale: Concerns absence of reports of system-caused discrepancies, not contractual allocation.
edges.quote: I have no recollection of any other Post Office branches reporting discrepancies caused by the Horizon system at this time

edges.rationale: Notes an allegation of Horizon error in a case but does not address contractual loss allocation.
edges.quote: the functionality of the Horizon System would be an issue

edges.rationale: Records a claim that Horizon caused losses but does not address how the contract allocated responsibility.
edges.quote: Mrs Wolstenholme claimed that the Horizon system itself caused losses in the sub post office accounts.

edges.rationale: Concerns whether Horizon caused losses factually, not the contractual allocation of responsibility.
edges.quote: she had been unable to identify any basis upon which the Horizon system could have caused the losses at the Marine Drive branch.

edges.rationale: Addresses lack of information about bugs, not how the contract allocated responsibility for system losses.
edges.quote: I do not recollect being informed by POL or Fujitsu of such issues.

edges.rationale: Asserts the system was not shown to cause losses but does not speak to the contractual scope of responsibility.
edges.quote: in none of the cases [in the Scheme] has our computer system been shown to have caused the losses complained of

edges.rationale: Addresses system development responsibility between POCL and Pathway, not the subpostmaster loss allocation.
edges.quote: It was Pathway and not POCL's responsibility to develop their system in accordance with the requirements of private finance.
